dna_damage.interpretation: High radiation exposure and long mission duration can increase predicted stress on DNA repair systems.
dna_damage.why_it_matters: DNA damage response pathways are important for genome stability and long-term cancer risk monitoring.

mitochondrial_stress.interpretation: Mission stressors may disrupt mitochondrial energy production and increase oxidative stress.
mitochondrial_stress.why_it_matters: Mitochondrial dysfunction is linked to fatigue, immune changes, aging biology, and cellular stress.

immune_dysfunction.interpretation: Long-duration spaceflight may alter immune regulation, especially under stress and reduced physiological stability.
immune_dysfunction.why_it_matters: Immune dysregulation can affect infection response, inflammation, and recovery from cellular damage.

inflammation.interpretation: Radiation and prolonged environmental stress may increase inflammatory signaling.
inflammation.why_it_matters: Chronic inflammation is connected to tissue stress, immune imbalance, and aging-related disease pathways.

senescence.interpretation: Long missions with elevated radiation may increase predicted aging-associated cellular stress.
senescence.why_it_matters: Senescence is relevant to tissue aging, inflammation, cancer biology, and long-term astronaut health.
